prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is in black and white and unfolds from an action scene into a product-focused display.

At the beginning, the frame shows an outdoor athletic setting, possibly a playing field. Several figures are visible near a horizontal line or barrier across the lower part of the image. They appear to be wearing dark uniforms and helmets or protective headgear, suggesting a sports activity, though the exact sport is not fully clear from the sampled frame. A light-colored ball or object is visible above the field area.

Later, the scene changes to a close-up promotional composition. A smiling child is shown in the background, partially obscured by product images in the foreground. The product shown is labeled “JUVENAL,” with text reading “LIQUID VITAMINS and IRON” and “NUTRITIONAL SUPPLEMENT.” Both a rectangular carton and a bottle are visible, appearing as a superimposed or studio-style product display over the child’s face. The lighting creates striped shadows across the scene.

By the final frame, the child is no longer visible. The commercial ends on a clear product shot: a boxed package of “JUVENAL LIQUID VITAMINS and IRON” stands on the left, and a dark glass bottle with a white label stands on the right. The label again reads “JUVENAL” and “NUTRITIONAL SUPPLEMENT.” The products are arranged on a tabletop or display surface against a simple studio background.
